root_text.an5.98:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu ānāpānassatiṁ bahulīkaronto nacirasseva akuppaṁ paṭivijjhati.
root_text.an5.98:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu appaṭṭho hoti appakicco subharo susantoso jīvitaparikkhāresu;
root_text.an5.98:1.8: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu ānāpānassatiṁ bahulīkaronto nacirasseva akuppaṁ paṭivijjhatī”ti.

translation_text.an5.98:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant practicing mindfulness of breathing who has five things will soon penetrate the unshakable.
translation_text.an5.98:1.3: It’s when a mendicant has few obligations and duties, and is unburdensome and contented with life’s necessities.
translation_text.an5.98:1.4: They eat little, not devoted to filling their stomach.
translation_text.an5.98:1.5: They are rarely drowsy, and are dedicated to wakefulness.
translation_text.an5.98:1.6: They live in the wilderness, in remote lodgings.
translation_text.an5.98:1.7: They review the extent of their mind’s freedom.
translation_text.an5.98:1.8: A mendicant practicing mindfulness of breathing who has these five things will soon penetrate the unshakable.”
